message:
feat(build): Implement dsr build command with orchestration

- Add cmd_build() with full argument parsing (--tool, --target, --targets,
  --version, --output-dir, --parallel, --resume, --allow-dirty)
- Source act_runner.sh module for build orchestration
- Integrate with act_orchestrate_build for Linux builds via act
- Support SSH-based builds for macOS/Windows hosts
- Generate manifests with act_generate_manifest
- Output JSON or human-readable build summary
- Check for dirty working tree (with --allow-dirty override)
- Detect version from version files when not specified
- Validate tool configs from repos.d/

Closes bd-1jt.2.1

Also closes:
- bd-1jt.2.2: Linux build environment (trj) complete
- bd-1jt.2.3: macOS build environment (mmini) complete
- bd-1jt.2.5: act installation on trj complete
- bd-1jt.2.7: Windows build environment (wlap) partial

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.8","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management (XDG + Schema Validation)

## Purpose
Centralize configuration so every command uses the same sources of truth for repos, hosts, secrets, and defaults. Consistent config prevents drift and makes automation reproducible.

## XDG Layout
- `~/.config/dsr/`
  - `config.yaml` (global defaults + schema_version)
  - `repos.yaml` (tool registry + build matrix)
  - `hosts.yaml` (build hosts + capabilities)
  - `notifications.yaml`
- `~/.cache/dsr/` (API cache, downloads)
- `~/.local/state/dsr/` (logs, state, run history)

## Precedence Rules
1. CLI flags
2. Environment variables (`DSR_*`)
3. Config files
4. Built-in defaults

## repos.yaml Schema (minimum fields)
- `tool`, `repo`, `local_path`
- `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (pre-release gates)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`

## hosts.yaml Schema
- host aliases, platform, capabilities, concurrency limits
- ssh options (connect timeout, identity)

## Migration & Versioning
- `schema_version` required
- `dsr config migrate` upgrades older schemas
- Unknown keys rejected unless `--allow-unknown`

## Commands
```bash
dsr config init
 dsr config show [key]
 dsr config validate
 dsr config set KEY=VALUE
 dsr config edit
 dsr config migrate
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config loads at startup with deterministic precedence
- [ ] Validation catches missing/invalid keys
- [ ] Defaults are sane and documented
- [ ] `--json` outputs resolved config for automation
- [ ] yq-based edits are safe; fallback mode is read-only","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:27.935807062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:12.840449840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:47:07.588658283Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr config command with init/show/get/set/validate/edit subcommands. Created src/config.sh module and main dsr CLI. XDG-compliant paths, JSON output support, precedence rules (CLI > env > config > defaults). Also implemented dsr doctor for diagnostics.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:33:56.274007404Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:38:53.251178068Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-8cg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T15:50:48.532928914Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.9","title":"Implement GitHub API adapter with caching + rate-limit handling","description":"# Implement GitHub API Adapter with Caching + Rate-Limit Handling

## Purpose
Centralize all GitHub API access so commands are consistent, cached, and resilient to rate limits.

## Responsibilities
- Prefer gh api when available (auth + pagination)
- Fallback to curl + GITHUB_TOKEN
- Support ETag / If-None-Match caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/
- Normalize errors into a common JSON shape
- Expose helpers for workflow runs, releases, tags, and compare endpoints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unified helper used by dsr check/watch/release
- [ ] API responses cached with TTL + ETag
- [ ] Rate-limit errors trigger backoff + clear messages
- [ ] All errors map to global exit codes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413349290Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413268548Z","close_reason":"Implemented GitHub API adapter in src/github.sh with: gh_api() for unified API access (gh CLI or curl fallback), ETag caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/, rate-limit handling with backoff, and helpers for workflow_runs, releases, tags, compare, and asset upload.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2","title":"Theme 2: Local Build Infrastructure","description":"# Local Build Infrastructure

## Purpose
Build binaries locally when GH Actions is throttled, using available hardware:
- Linux builds on Threadripper workstation (trj, local)
- macOS builds on Mac mini (mmini)
- Windows builds on Windows laptop (wlap) or cross-compilation fallback

## Build Hosts

### Threadripper Workstation (trj)
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Linux x86_64, Linux ARM64 (via cross), Windows (via cross)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun, Docker/act

### Mac mini (mmini)
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS
- Arch: ARM64 (Apple Silicon)
- Can build: macOS ARM64 (native), macOS x86_64 (via arch -x86_64)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

### Windows laptop (wlap)
- Connection: ssh wlap (via Tailscale)
- OS: Windows 10/11
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Windows x86_64 (native)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

## Cross-Compilation Strategy
For targets we can't build natively:
- Rust: cargo-zigbuild or cross for Windows/ARM
- Go: GOOS/GOARCH environment variables
- Bun: bun compile with target flag

## Key Commands
```bash
dsr build ntm                       # Build for all targets
dsr build ntm --target linux-amd64  # Specific target
dsr build ntm --host trj            # Force specific build host
```

## Target Matrix (example)

| Tool | Language | Linux x64 | Linux ARM | macOS ARM | macOS x64 | Windows |
|------|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| ntm  | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| cass | Rust     | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| bv   | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| ...  | ...      | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...     |

## Build Output
Artifacts + logs live under the state directory:
- `~/.local/state/dsr/artifacts/<tool>/<version>/<target>/`
- `~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/` (per-host logs + metadata)

## Dependencies
- SSH access to build hosts (mmini, wlap)
- Toolchains installed on each host
- Docker + act available on trj for Linux workflows
- Sufficient disk space (~5GB per full build)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Host names and mappings align with `hosts.yaml` (trj/mmini/wlap)
- [ ] Artifacts and logs land in state directory with a manifest
- [ ] Cross-compilation paths documented for Rust/Go/Bun
- [ ] Non-interactive builds run without prompts","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:51:40.191796583Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1jt.2.1","title":"Implement dsr build command - local compilation","description":"# Implement dsr build command — local + remote compilation

## Purpose
Compile a tool locally or on remote build hosts, producing release-ready binaries with a manifest and logs.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr build <tool>
 dsr build <tool> --target <target>
 dsr build <tool> --host <host>
 dsr build <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr build <tool> --output-dir <dir>
 dsr build <tool> --parallel
 dsr build <tool> --resume
```

## Build Flow (deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)
2. Acquire lock + create run_id
3. Select host per target (health + capability matrix)
4. Build via act for Linux jobs; native builds for macOS/Windows
5. Collect artifacts to state dir + manifest
6. Emit JSON summary

## Implementation Notes
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations (no global cd)
- Use `ssh <host> "<cmd>"` with explicit error capture
- Use rsync/scp with retries for artifact collection
- Use manifest schema as source of truth for release

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds all targets for Rust/Go/Bun tools
- [ ] Artifacts named per manifest contract
- [ ] Logs captured per host
- [ ] Parallel builds do not collide
- [ ] Resume skips completed  {"id":"bd-1jt.2.10","title":"Validate GoReleaser config compatibility for each tool","description":"# GoReleaser Config Validation

## Purpose
Ensure dsr can replicate GoReleaser outputs (targets, archives, naming) so fallback builds match official releases.

## Approach
- Prefer `goreleaser release --snapshot --skip-publish` when available
- Fallback: parse .goreleaser.yaml via yq
- Compare produced artifacts against expected naming template

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Targets and archive formats match GoReleaser config
- [ ] Naming templates produce identical asset names
- [ ] Fallback parsing works when goreleaser not installed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:21.603418246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:48.748847712Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:21.603418246Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:49.305063723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.11","title":"Implement build workspace isolation + lock/state machine","description":"# Implement Build Workspace Isolation + Lock/State Machine

## Purpose
Prevent overlapping builds and enable reliable resume/retry by persisting build state per tool/version.

## Key Behaviors
- Acquire lock per tool+version (state dir)
- Create unique run_id + workspace (no overwrites)
- Persist state JSON with per-host status + artifacts
- Resume uses state to skip completed hosts

## Requirements
- Explicit error handling (no `set -e`)
- State updated after every host step
- Locks released on clean exit or timeout
- Stale lock detection (PID + timestamp + TTL)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Concurrent runs are blocked or queued deterministically
- [ ] State file created and updated per host step
- [ ] --resume continues from last known state
- [ ] Logs reference run_id + state path
- [ ] Lock files are atomic and auto-cleaned when stale","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024490854Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024473411Z","close_reason":"Implemented build workspace isolation with lock/state machine in src/build_state.sh. 16 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.12","title":"Define artifact naming + build manifest schema","description":"# Define Artifact Naming + Build Manifest Schema

## Purpose
Standardize artifact names and produce a manifest JSON that release/install/tests can trust.

## Naming Contract
- `{tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch}[.exe]`
- `checksums.sha256` includes all binaries
- Optional `.minisig` for each artifact

## Manifest Schema (minimum)
- `tool`, `version`, `run_id`, `git_sha`, `schema_version`
- `artifacts[]`: name, target, sha256, size_bytes, signature_path
- `hosts[]`: host, status, duration_ms, log_path, retry_count
- `build_env`: toolchain versions, workflow/job metadata, act version

## Placement
- `~/.local/state/dsr/manifests/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>.json`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest produced for every build
- [ ] Release + installer use manifest as source of truth
- [ ] Tests validate manifest schema
- [ ] Manifest includes toolchain + host metadata for auditability","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:45.903938160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:54:50.125203319Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:49:35.088175561Z","close_reason":"Created schemas/manifest.json (JSON Schema for build manifests), docs/ARTIFACT_NAMING.md (comprehensive naming convention docs), and scripts/tests/fixtures/manifest-example.json (test fixture). Defines {tool}-{version}-{os}-{arch} naming, manifest structure with artifacts, hosts, signatures, and SBOM references.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.12","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:45.903938160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -96,7 +96,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-36r","title":"Unit tests for quality_gates.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for quality_gates.sh Module

## Module Overview
quality_gates.sh provides pre-release quality checks:
- `quality_run_checks` - Execute all configured checks
- `quality_parse_config` - Read checks from repos.d/*.yaml
- `quality_format_results` - Output results in JSON/human formats
- `quality_should_skip` - Handle --skip-checks flag

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Check Execution Tests
- Run single check command (e.g., `cargo test`)
- Run multiple checks in sequence
- Capture stdout/stderr per check
- Measure duration per check
- Handle check timeout

### 2. Config Parsing Tests
- Parse checks array from YAML
- Handle missing checks section (no-op)
- Validate check command format
- Support check-specific options

### 3. Result Formatting Tests
- JSON output matches schema
- Human output includes pass/fail indicators
- Duration shown per check
- Exit code summary at end

### 4. Skip Logic Tests
- --skip-checks skips all checks
- --skip-checks=lint skips specific check
- --dry-run shows planned checks
- Non-interactive mode continues on failure

### 5. Error Handling Tests
- Check command not found → clear error
- Check returns non-zero → record failure
- Timeout reached → record timeout
- Continue after failure (report at end)

## Test Approach
- Create temp repos.d with test configs
- Use simple check commands (true, false, sleep)
- Verify JSON schema compliance
- Test all exit code scenarios","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:56.002943319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:48:13.544549533Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-36r","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:56.002943319Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-39w","title":"E2E: dsr doctor (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr doctor (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify doctor checks real dependencies and produces actionable output.

## Approach
- Run doctor in a clean env and capture missing deps.
- Run with `--json` to validate schema and stream separation.

## Logging
- Log detected deps and their status.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Missing deps are reported clearly.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.
- [ ] Non-interactive mode does not prompt.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:15.972960698Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:52.165070220Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-39w","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:50.126884944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-39w","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:59:52.163395945Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3cn","title":"Unit tests for github.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for github.sh Module

## Module Overview
github.sh provides GitHub API access with caching and rate limiting:
- `gh_check` - Verify gh CLI + authentication
- `gh_check_token` - Validate GITHUB_TOKEN
- `gh_api` - Make API requests with caching
- `gh_workflow_runs` - List workflow runs
- `gh_releases` - List releases
- `gh_create_release` - Create a release
- `gh_upload_asset` - Upload release asset

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Authentication Tests
- `gh_check` succeeds when gh is authenticated
- `gh_check` fails gracefully when gh not installed
- `gh_check` fails gracefully when not authenticated
- `gh_check_token` validates GITHUB_TOKEN format
- `gh_check_token` detects expired/invalid tokens (via API call)

### 2. Caching Tests
- Cache directory created under XDG_CACHE_HOME
- First request creates cache entry
- Second request within TTL uses cache
- Request after TTL expiry fetches fresh
- ETag-based validation (304 Not Modified)
- Cache key includes endpoint parameters

### 3. Rate Limiting Tests
- Rate limit detection via response headers
- Retry with backoff on 429 response
- Maximum retry count enforced (3)
- Rate limit warning logged

### 4. Error Handling Tests
- Network errors produce clear messages
- 404 responses handled correctly
- 401 (auth) errors trigger re-auth hint
- 500 errors trigger retry logic

### 5. JSON Output Tests
- `--json` mode returns valid JSON
- Error responses follow envelope schema
- Success responses include metadata

## Test Approach
- Test validation/formatting logic WITHOUT live API calls
- Use skip markers for tests requiring network
- Cache tests use temp directory
- Rate limit tests use mock time if needed","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:45.881678501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:36:22.064838117Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3cn","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:33:45.881678501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3ib","title":"E2E: dsr help/version smoke tests","description":"# E2E: dsr help/version smoke tests

## Purpose
Ensure CLI help/version output works across commands and respects --json.

## Scope
- `dsr --help`, `dsr <cmd> --help`
- `dsr --version` with and without --json

## Logging
- Capture stdout/stderr to verify stream separation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Version JSON is valid and on stdout only.
- [ ] No unexpected stderr output on success.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:57:09.741541708Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:11.423957059Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ib","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:09.839471386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3ib","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:11.423248314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3je","title":"E2E: dsr quality (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr quality (real behavior)

## Purpose
Exercise quality gate execution using real commands (true/false/sleep) and real repos.d config.

## Approach
- Create temp repos config with simple check commands.
- Run `dsr quality <tool>` and verify results and exit codes.
- Validate --json output schema and stderr separation.

## Logging
- Log the check commands executed and timing.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pass/fail/skip behaviors are correct.
- [ ] Durations recorded in output.
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:56:57.748351674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:08.395739440Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3je","depends_on_id":"bd-36r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:07.038078100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3je","depends_on_id":"bd-f97","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:08.395293210Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 cmd_build() {
-    log_warn "build command not yet implemented"
-    log_info "See bead bd-1jt.2.1 for implementation plan"
-    return 4
+    local tool_name=""
+    local targets=()
+    local version=""
+    local output_dir=""
+    # shellcheck disable=SC2034  # parallel reserved for future parallel build support
+    local parallel=false
+    # shellcheck disable=SC2034  # resume reserved for future resume support
+    local resume=false
+    local allow_dirty=false
+
+    # Parse arguments
+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+        case "$1" in
+            --tool|-t)
+                tool_name="$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --target)
+                targets+=("$2")
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --targets)
+                # Allow comma-separated list
+                IFS=',' read -ra targets <<< "$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --version|-V)
+                version="$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --output-dir|-o)
+                output_dir="$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --parallel|-p)
+                parallel=true  # shellcheck disable=SC2034
+                shift
+                ;;
+            --resume|-r)
+                resume=true  # shellcheck disable=SC2034
+                shift
+                ;;
+            --allow-dirty)
+                allow_dirty=true
+                shift
+                ;;
+            --help|-h)
+                cat << 'EOF'
+dsr build - Build artifacts locally
+
+USAGE:
+    dsr build <tool> [options]
+    dsr build --tool <name> [options]
+
+OPTIONS:
+    -t, --tool <name>      Tool to build (required, or as first positional arg)
+    --target <target>      Specific target (e.g., linux/amd64); can repeat
+    --targets <list>       Comma-separated target list
+    -V, --version <ver>    Version to build (default: detect from version files)
+    -o, --output-dir <dir> Output directory for artifacts
+    -p, --parallel         Build targets in parallel
+    -r, --resume           Resume interrupted build
+    --allow-dirty          Allow building with uncommitted changes
+
+DESCRIPTION:
+    Builds a tool for all configured targets using act (Linux) and
+    SSH to remote hosts (macOS/Windows).
+
+TARGETS:
+    Configured per tool in ~/.config/dsr/repos.d/<tool>.yaml
+    Common targets: linux/amd64, linux/arm64, darwin/arm64, windows/amd64
+
+EXAMPLES:
+    dsr build ntm                           Build ntm for all targets
+    dsr build ntm --target linux/amd64      Build only for Linux x64
+    dsr build ntm --version 1.2.3           Build specific version
+    dsr build ntm --parallel                Build all targets in parallel
+    dsr build --tool bv --resume            Resume interrupted bv build
+
+EXIT CODES:
+    0  - All builds succeeded
+    1  - Some builds failed
+    2  - Build already in progress (lock held)
+    4  - Invalid arguments or missing config
+    6  - Build failed
+EOF
+                return 0
+                ;;
+            -*)
+                log_error "Unknown option: $1"
+                log_info "Run 'dsr build --help' for usage"
+                return 4
+                ;;
+            *)
+                # First positional arg is tool name
+                if [[ -z "$tool_name" ]]; then
+                    tool_name="$1"
+                else
+                    # Additional positional args might be targets
+                    targets+=("$1")
+                fi
+                shift
+                ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    # Validate tool name
+    if [[ -z "$tool_name" ]]; then
+        log_error "Tool name required"
+        log_info "Run 'dsr build --help' for usage"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    # Set output directory default
+    local state_dir="${DSR_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/dsr}"
+    [[ -z "$output_dir" ]] && output_dir="$state_dir/artifacts"
+    mkdir -p "$output_dir"
+
+    # Check if act is available
+    if ! act_check; then
+        log_error "act not available - cannot build"
+        return 3
+    fi
+
+    # Load tool config
+    if ! act_load_repo_config "$tool_name"; then
+        log_error "Tool '$tool_name' not found in repos.d"
+        log_info "Available tools: $(act_list_tools 2>/dev/null | tr '\n' ' ')"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    # Get local path
+    local local_path
+    local_path=$(act_get_local_path "$tool_name")
+    if [[ -z "$local_path" || ! -d "$local_path" ]]; then
+        log_error "Local path not found for $tool_name: $local_path"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    # Check for dirty working tree
+    if ! $allow_dirty; then
+        if git_ops_is_dirty "$local_path"; then
+            log_error "Working tree has uncommitted changes in $local_path"
+            log_info "Commit changes or use --allow-dirty to override"
+            return 1
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Detect version if not specified
+    if [[ -z "$version" ]]; then
+        log_info "Detecting version from version files..."
+        if version=$(version_detect "$local_path" 2>/dev/null); then
+            log_info "Detected version: $version"
+        else
+            log_error "Cannot detect version. Specify with --version"
+            return 4
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Normalize version (ensure it has 'v' prefix for tag)
+    local tag
+    tag=$(git_ops_version_to_tag "$version")
+
+    # Check if tag exists
+    if ! git_ops_tag_exists "$local_path" "$tag"; then
+        log_warn "Tag $tag does not exist in $local_path"
+        log_info "Creating tag with: dsr version tag $tool_name"
+        # For now, proceed anyway - the version might be in version files but not tagged
+    fi
+
+    log_info "Building $tool_name $version"
+    log_set_tool "$tool_name"
+
+    # Get targets from args or config
+    local target_list
+    if [[ ${#targets[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+        target_list="${targets[*]}"
+    else
+        target_list=$(act_get_targets "$tool_name")
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$target_list" ]]; then
+        log_error "No targets configured for $tool_name"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    log_info "Targets: $target_list"
+
+    # Record start time
+    local start_time
+    start_time=$(date +%s)
+
+    # Run orchestrated build
+    local result
+    # shellcheck disable=SC2086
+    result=$(act_orchestrate_build "$tool_name" "$version" $target_list 2>&1)
+    local exit_code=$?
+
+    # Parse result
+    local end_time duration
+    end_time=$(date +%s)
+    duration=$((end_time - start_time))
+
+    # Extract summary from result
+    local total success failed
+    total=$(echo "$result" | grep -o '"total":[0-9]*' | grep -o '[0-9]*' | tail -1)
+    success=$(echo "$result" | grep -o '"success":[0-9]*' | grep -o '[0-9]*' | tail -1)
+    failed=$(echo "$result" | grep -o '"failed":[0-9]*' | grep -o '[0-9]*' | tail -1)
+
+    # Default values if parsing failed
+    [[ -z "$total" ]] && total=0
+    [[ -z "$success" ]] && success=0
+    [[ -z "$failed" ]] && failed=0
+
+    # Generate manifest
+    local manifest_file="$output_dir/${tool_name}-${version}-manifest.json"
+    if [[ $success -gt 0 ]]; then
+        # Generate manifest from results
+        act_generate_manifest "$tool_name" "$version" "$output_dir" > "$manifest_file" 2>/dev/null || true
+    fi
+
+    # Output
+    if $JSON_MODE; then
+        local build_details
+        build_details=$(cat << EOF
+{
+  "tool": "$tool_name",
+  "version": "$version",
+  "targets": $(echo "$target_list" | tr ' ' '\n' | jq -R . | jq -s .),
+  "total": $total,
+  "success": $success,
+  "failed": $failed,
+  "duration_seconds": $duration,
+  "output_dir": "$output_dir",
+  "manifest": "$manifest_file"
+}
+EOF
+)
+        if [[ $failed -eq 0 && $success -gt 0 ]]; then
+            json_envelope "build" "success" 0 "$build_details"
+        elif [[ $success -gt 0 ]]; then
+            json_envelope "build" "partial" 1 "$build_details"
+        else
+            json_envelope "build" "failed" 6 "$build_details"
+        fi
+    else
+        echo ""
+        log_info "=== Build Summary ==="
+        log_info "Tool:     $tool_name"
+        log_info "Version:  $version"
+        log_info "Duration: ${duration}s"
+        echo ""
+        log_info "Results:"
+        log_info "  Total:    $total"
+        if [[ $success -gt 0 ]]; then
+            log_ok "  Success:  $success"
+        else
+            log_info "  Success:  $success"
+        fi
+        if [[ $failed -gt 0 ]]; then
+            log_error "  Failed:   $failed"
+        else
+            log_info "  Failed:   $failed"
+        fi
+        echo ""
+        if [[ -f "$manifest_file" ]]; then
+            log_ok "Manifest: $manifest_file"
+        fi
+        log_info "Artifacts: $output_dir"
+    fi
+
+    # Return appropriate exit code
+    if [[ $failed -eq 0 && $success -gt 0 ]]; then
+        return 0
+    elif [[ $success -gt 0 ]]; then
+        return 1  # Partial success
+    else
+        return 6  # All failed
+    fi
 }
 
 # Quality gates command - run pre-release checks
